mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals imminent easing in discount rates and liquidity conditions, which raises equity present values and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months when FEDFUNDS is falling on a 3-month basis, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation print that keeps running above the policy rate implies real rates are restrictive and disinflation progress is insufficient, which compresses valuation multiples and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises faster than FEDFUNDS for 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal loses sign consistency after controlling for FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above recent levels marks weakening labor demand and higher recession probability, which pressures earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative in at least 55% of threshold-crossing episodes, or if the effect disappears in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: A broad decline in RSAFS indicates weaker consumer spending demand, which lowers expected revenue growth and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if negative RSAFS momentum fails to align with negative 20-day SPX returns more than half the time, or if the rolling Rank IC is below 0.03 for any 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO together with stable employment signals a real activity upswing without immediate labor slack, supporting earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises while PAYEMS is non-declining over the same 2-month window
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not deliver positive 60-day returns in at least 55% of cases, or if INDPRO alone explains the sign better than the joint condition.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in HOUST signals housing demand and credit transmission remain healthy, which supports cyclical growth expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods with positive HOUST momentum, or if the effect vanishes once CPIAUCSL is added.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is weak and RSAFS is also soft, the economy is losing final-demand momentum, which should reduce earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth is below its prior 4-quarter average and RSAFS is negative on a 3-month basis
failure_condition: Reject if the joint slowdown condition fails to predict negative 60-day returns in at least 60% of samples, or if either proxy alone delivers the same sign with no incremental lift.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a low UNRATE combined with still-high FEDFUNDS means policy is restrictive relative to labor market slack, so valuation pressure dominates and S&P 500 returns are negative over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not negative during periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% and UNRATE below 4.5%, or if the joint condition loses significance after 12 months.
